name: Tierra Altas alta de Sória y San Pedro Manrique — circular desde Yaguas
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Yanguas are characterized by the region's highlands and varied terrain, offering challenging ascents and scenic descents. The landscape features rolling hills and elevated sections, providing extensive views across the Soria province. Road cyclists can expect routes that navigate through rural areas and past historic villages, with significant elevation gains. The area's topography makes it suitable for those seeking longer, more demanding rides. Best road cycling routes around Yanguas The most popular road cycling route is Soria Highlands Loop, a 53.1 miles (85.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 14 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial climbs through the Soria highlands. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sierra de Alcarama – Cornago Loop, a difficult 76.9 miles (123.7 km) path. This extensive route traverses the Sierra de Alcarama, known for its demanding gradients and remote landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the Tierra Altas alta de Sória y San Pedro Manrique — circular desde Yaguas, a 38.4 miles (61.8 km) trail leading through the high plains of Soria, often completed in about 3 hours 8 minutes. Road cycling around Yanguas is defined by high plains, challenging ascents, and rural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, including several difficult and moderate routes. The routes in Yanguas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 185 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Yanguas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Yanguas?
A: There are over 8 road cycling routes around Yanguas, offering a variety of distances and elevations for different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Yanguas?
A: Road cycling routes around Yanguas are characterized by highlands and varied terrain, featuring rolling hills and elevated sections. You can expect challenging ascents and scenic descents, providing extensive views across the Soria province. The area's topography is suitable for those seeking longer, more demanding rides through rural areas and past historic villages.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Yanguas?
A: While Yanguas is known for its challenging terrain, there are 5 moderate routes available. These routes offer a good balance for those looking for a less strenuous ride compared to the more difficult options, but still provide a rewarding experience in the region's landscape.

Q: What are some of the more challenging road cycling routes near Yanguas?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990927883/soria-highlands-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Soria Highlands Loop&lt;/a&gt; (53.1 miles with substantial climbs) or the extensive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1078451348/sierra-de-alcarama-cornago-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Alcarama – Cornago Loop&lt;/a&gt; (76.9 miles with demanding gradients) offer significant elevation gains and remote landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Yanguas?
A: Given the region's elevated terrain and potential for varied weather, the best seasons for road cycling in Yanguas are typically late spring, summer, and early autumn. During these months, you can generally expect more stable weather conditions and pleasant temperatures for longer rides. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.

Q: What attractions can I see along the road cycling routes in Yanguas?
A: Many routes pass through or near interesting points. You might encounter the historic charm of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2718792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yanguas Historic Village&lt;/a&gt; itself, or the settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6373808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pedro Manrique&lt;/a&gt;. The region also offers natural highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5824640" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Barranco Perdido Paleo Adventure Park&lt;/a&gt;, which can be a point of interest during your ride.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Yanguas?
A: Yes, many of the routes around Yanguas are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1078408892/tierra-altas-alta-de-soria-y-san-pedro-manrique-circular-desde-yaguas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tierra Altas alta de Sória y San Pedro Manrique — circular desde Yaguas&lt;/a&gt; is a 38.4-mile circular route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31851241" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yanguas Historic Village – Yanguas loop from Yanguas&lt;/a&gt; is another moderate circular option.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Yanguas?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the challenging ascents, the extensive views across the Soria province, and the opportunity to cycle through remote rural landscapes and past historic villages.

Q: Is parking available near the road cycling routes in Yanguas?
A: Yanguas is a rural area, and parking is generally available within or near the village centers, which often serve as starting points for routes. Specific parking information for individual routes may vary, but you can typically find suitable spots in the villages themselves.

Q: Are there any road bike rental services near Yanguas?
A: As Yanguas is a smaller, more remote location, dedicated road bike rental shops might not be directly in the village. It is advisable to check in larger nearby towns or cities in the Soria province, or to arrange rentals in advance from a specialized provider if you plan to cycle in the area.

Q: What essential gear should I bring for road cycling in Yanguas?
A: Given the challenging terrain and varied conditions, essential gear includes a well-maintained road bike, a helmet, appropriate cycling apparel (including layers for changing weather), sufficient water and nutrition, a repair kit, and a GPS device or map for navigation. Due to the remote nature of some routes, carrying extra supplies is recommended.

Q: Are there cafes or refreshment stops along the road cycling routes?
A: While some routes pass through small villages, dedicated cafes or refreshment stops might be sparse on more remote sections. It's recommended to plan your route to include stops in larger villages like Yanguas or San Pedro Manrique, or to carry enough provisions for your ride, especially on longer loops.
tips:
Santa Cruz de Yanguas is a municipality in Soria in the Tierras Altas region, community of Castilla y León. During the Middle Ages it was part of the Community of Villa y Tierra de Yanguas.

It is located in the Baos River basin, and has important forest and mycological resources. The municipal area is part of the Urbión Regional Reserve, with a large presence of deer, roe deer, wild boar and woodcock.

Of its heritage, the Roman bridge that spans the bed of the Baos River, the church of the Santísima Trinidad (16th century) in Gothic style and the hermitage of the Virgen del Pilar stand out. As in the rest of the region, you can find dinosaur footprints (ichnites).

Population that is located to the south of the Sierra de San Cristrobal and that is crossed by the Río Linaris. The town, in the middle of the Iberian System, is located in the place called Tierras Altas.
This town is located at the northern end of the Sierra Soriana, near La Rioja, on one of the hills of the Serra de Cameros.
